# HIPPMEM

HIPPMEM is a native association memory engine for AI agents, written in Rust.
It discovers associations at write time, retrieves via spreading activation, and evolves through Hebbian learning and decay.

## Getting Started
- Quickstart (5 min): docs/quickstart.md — clone, build, write, retrieve, explain
- User Guide (20 min): docs/user-guide.md — concepts, lifecycle, CLI, Rust API, FAQ
- Cookbook: docs/cookbook.md — 7 copy-paste scenario recipes with runnable code
- Concepts deep-dive: docs/concepts.md — MemoryUnit, associations, spreading activation, consolidation
- API Reference: docs/api-reference.md — all 7 Engine method signatures, types, error codes
- gRPC Guide: docs/grpc-guide.md — Python/Go/Node.js client examples
- Configuration: docs/configuration.md — AlgoParams, EngineConfig, BackendSelection, tuning tips
- Comparison: docs/comparison.md — vs vector DBs, graph DBs, Mem0, Graphiti

## Architecture
9 Rust crates in a workspace:
- hippmem-core: data model, newtypes, Clock/Rng, config, error types
- hippmem-store: redb persistence, Tantivy BM25, HNSW semantic index, inverted indexes
- hippmem-model: Embedder/Extractor/Reranker/Summarizer traits + deterministic fallback + API backends
- hippmem-write: write path — keys, candidate discovery, scoring, edge building, staged pipeline
- hippmem-retrieval: retrieve path — seeds, energy, spreading, rerank, explain
- hippmem-consolidation: Hebbian, decay, compaction, summarization, background worker
- hippmem-engine: assembly layer — Engine struct + 7 public APIs + CLI + gRPC server
- hippmem-eval: evaluation framework — 5 baselines, 10 task types, 50 fixtures
- hippmem-grpc: gRPC proto + tonic service (feature-gated)

## Key Commands
```bash
cargo build --bin hippmem          # CLI
cargo build --bin hippmem-server   # gRPC server
cargo test --workspace             # all tests (deterministic fallback, no network)
cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  # gate
```

## Public API Summary
Engine::open(config) -> Engine
Engine::close(self)
engine.write(WriteMemoryInput) -> WriteMemoryOutput
engine.retrieve(RetrieveInput) -> RetrieveOutput
engine.explain(MemoryId, context) -> Explanation
engine.consolidate(ConsolidationScope) -> ConsolidationReport
engine.inspect(InspectQuery) -> InspectReport
engine.feedback(FeedbackInput) -> ()

## Key Design Decisions
- Write-time association discovery (not just retrieval-time)
- Spreading activation with edge-type modifiers (not vector similarity ranking)
- Deterministic fallback backend (works offline, no API keys needed)
- Native graph traversal (self-implemented, not delegated to graph DB)
- All state in one redb file + Tantivy directory

## Links
- Architecture: docs/architecture/whitepaper.md — design philosophy and first principles
